concept.title: Project Approach
concept.shortdesc: This topic describes the end-to-end documentation approach, from understanding the requirements to delivering the final content. It also outlines how the documentation was reviewed using a structured checklist aligned with the Microsoft Technical Writing Style Guide to ensure clarity, consistency, and usability.

conbody.p: This topic outlines how the documentation for this assignment was created, from initial setup to final review.
The approach focused on building both the content and the documentation system, while staying aligned with the constraints of the assignment.

section.title: 1. Understanding the assignment
section.p: The process began by reviewing the assignment requirements and identifying:

ul.li: available source material, such as screenshots and video
constraints, such as not assuming functionality beyond what is shown

section.p: This helped define the scope before any writing began.

section.title: 2. Structuring input and output
section.p: To keep the work organized, the setup was divided into:

ul.li: an input repository for source material
an output Docusaurus repository for final documentation

section.p: This separation made it easier to trace content back to its source and avoid mixing raw inputs with final output.

section.title: 3. Setting up the documentation framework
section.p: A Docusaurus site was initialized to host the documentation.
During setup, several issues were resolved, including:

ul.li: environment configuration, such as Node, npm, and terminal setup
project initialization within an existing repository
fixing configuration files such as sidebars.js and docusaurus.config.js

section.p: The goal was to create a clean, minimal documentation site focused on the assignment.

section.title: 4. Defining the documentation structure
section.p: The documentation was organized into task-based pages:
This ensured that each deliverable was clearly separated and easy to review.

section.title: 5. Writing from source evidence
section.p: All documentation was created by reviewing the provided screenshots and video.
Key decisions included:

ul.li: using UI labels exactly as shown

section.p: This ensured accuracy and alignment with the assignment expectations.

section.title: 6. Iterative refinement
section.p: Initial drafts were refined to improve:
Content was reorganized where needed to follow a clear pattern: What → When → How.

section.title: 7. Using AI tools thoughtfully
section.p: AI tools were used to support drafting, structuring, and troubleshooting.
However, all outputs were manually reviewed and adjusted to:

ul.li: align with the actual product behavior

section.p: AI was treated as a support tool, not a source of truth.

section.title: 8. Reviewing against style principles
section.p: All content was reviewed using a checklist based on Microsoft-style writing principles.
The focus was on:

section.title: 9. Refining the documentation site
section.p: The Docusaurus site was further customized to:

ul.li: align the visual presentation with a more modern documentation style

section.title: 10. Final validation
section.p: Before submission, the documentation was reviewed to ensure:

ul.li: correct rendering in the documentation site

section.title: Final note
section.p: This approach reflects not just the final documentation, but the process used to create it.
The focus was on building documentation that is:

ul.li: aligned with how real product documentation is developed
